rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions, using int, real, or bool as appropriate.
Avoid placing implementation-specific identifiers or bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for such constructs.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either declared in the same annex or are visible features of the component.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations to prevent type resolution errors.
Validate that guarantee expressions have consistent types on both sides of binary operators to avoid '<error>' type issues.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation contracts should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Component type annexes should define assumptions and guarantees that relate visible inputs to visible outputs, not implementation-only paths.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers in AGREE contracts; always ensure visibility or declaration before use.
Do not mix invalid or unresolvable types in expressions; align helper types with the expected expression result.
